coalitions: Moderate ASEAN bloc (Brunei-Malaysia-Indonesia) favoring bilateral CBMs and joint ventures
Hardline claimants (Philippines-Vietnam) backed by US-Japan-India insisting on binding UNCLOS resolution
note: The open ASEAN split over the Code of Conduct fractured claimant unity, letting China exploit the rift and Brunei's momentum steer a non-binding CBM/joint-venture framework that high mediator pressure forced through despite the confrontational mood, while no party moved off its sovereignty red line.
